passage: A team of environmental scientists compared air quality index (AQI) readings across six monitoring stations in a metropolitan area during summer 2024. They concluded that industrial zones consistently recorded worse air quality than residential zones.
stem: Which choice most effectively uses data from the figure to support the scientists' conclusion?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The three industrial-zone stations recorded average AQI readings of 142, 137, and 128, while the three residential-zone stations recorded averages of 68, 74, and 81.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Station 3, located in a residential zone, recorded an average AQI of 81, which was higher than Station 6's residential reading of 68.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Station 1, in an industrial zone, recorded an average AQI of 142, which was the highest reading among all six stations.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The average AQI across all six stations was 105, placing the metropolitan area's overall air quality in the 'unhealthy for sensitive groups' category.

explanation: The claim is that industrial zones consistently had worse air quality than residential zones. Choice A supports this by showing all three industrial stations (142, 137, 128) recorded higher AQI values than all three residential stations (68, 74, 81), demonstrating a consistent pattern. Choice B compares two residential stations to each other. Choice C only highlights the single highest station without comparing zones. Choice D provides an overall average that doesn't distinguish between zones.

distractorRationale.A: Correct: compares all industrial stations to all residential stations, showing every industrial reading exceeds every residential reading.
distractorRationale.B: Wrong (misread value): compares two residential stations to each other rather than comparing industrial to residential zones.
distractorRationale.C: Wrong (incomplete evidence): mentions only one industrial station's reading without comparing it to the residential zone pattern.
distractorRationale.D: Wrong (extrapolation error): calculates an overall average that doesn't appear in the raw data and doesn't distinguish between the two zone types.

imageDescription: Data table titled 'Summer 2024 Average AQI Readings by Monitoring Station.' Columns: Station, Zone Type, Average AQI. Rows: Station 1 - Industrial - 142; Station 2 - Industrial - 137; Station 4 - Industrial - 128; Station 3 - Residential - 81; Station 5 - Residential - 74; Station 6 - Residential - 68. Source: Metropolitan Air Quality Commission, 2024.

chartData.title: Summer 2024 Average AQI Readings by Monitoring Station

passageMetadata.source: Adapted from Metropolitan Air Quality Commission seasonal report
